if I'm in a directory and run pc archeologist and I select multiple paths, will the conversations from those paths will converge into the wiki of the current PWD?
No — routing is by each transcript's internal cwd field, not the current PWD. Each selected project's sessions write to its own separate wiki.
let's instead allow the user to select multiple directories and they all merge into the current project (for when sessions exist in different paths for the same project)
Implemented: interactive picker selections now route all sessions to the current PWD's wiki. The --yes and --project modes keep original per-session routing.
if I'm running from /Users/pablofernandez/Work/serial-killer you could put at the top and pre-selected the conversations that match in the list
Done — the picker now floats the current directory's matching projects to the top and pre-checks them, with all selections routing to the current wiki.

[Image #1] what I see in pc archeologist's window is utterly useless... I would like to see "I looked at conversation 123", "I discovered this fact", "Wrote guide abc", etc, etc. This needs a rethink of this UI to make it human-compatible, not a log of a bunch of bullshit
The advisor is right — I need to verify the data before building the UI. Two key checks first.

Completed the TUI redesign: feed now uses natural-language narration, pipeline-internal stages are hidden, a detail pane lets you open any feed line to see full content, and event payloads now include text excerpts, dates, and session IDs.
